# Experiment: 50%-duty TDM FS/LRCK generated by CLC10

Branch: `exp/tdm-master-clc10-fs50`

> **Status: VERIFIED on HW (2026-06-30).** RP70 shows a clean ~49.15 kHz / 50%-duty FS,
> BCLK ≈ 12.58 MHz, **BCLK/FS = 256**, TDM data continuous with `miss=0`. The CLC-bypass
> passthrough step confirmed the routing; the working FF mapping is CLK=Gate1, J=Gate2,
> K=Gate4, R=Gate3 (see below).

## Goal

The SPI1 TDM8 self-clocked master normally drives its frame sync (FRMSYNC = `SS1`) as a
short pulse once per 8-word audio frame. This experiment keeps BCLK and DATA exactly as
they are but **replaces the externally observed FS with a ~50%-duty signal built by
CLC10**, to check whether a clean I2S/TDM-style LRCK can be synthesized from the SPI
frame-sync marker. Waveform observation is the objective — audio fidelity is secondary.

## Idea

```
  SPI SCK   -----------------------------> external BCLK     (unchanged: SCK1 -> RP75)
  SPI SDO   -----------------------------> external DATA out (unchanged: SDO1 -> RP101)

  SPI FSYNC (SS1) -- short marker every 4 words (128 BCLK)
        | internal, via Virtual Pin RPV8 (no jumper)
        v
      CLC10  J-K flip-flop, J=K=1  ->  toggles on every marker
        |
        v
  CLC10OUT  -----------------------------> external FS/LRCK  (RP70, was SS1)

  SPI FSYNC marker : ^.......^.......^.......     every 128 BCLK
  CLC10 output FS  : ____--------____--------     256 BCLK period, ~50% duty
```

- 1 word = 32 BCLK, TDM8 frame = 8 words = 256 BCLK.
- FS pulse every 4 words = 128 BCLK -> one CLC toggle per half-frame -> FS period 256 BCLK.
- BCLK / FS ≈ 256, FS duty ≈ 50%.

## How it is wired (all facts from DS70005591C + p33AK512MPS512.h — nothing guessed)

| Piece | Setting | Source |
|---|---|---|
| SPI used | **SPI1** (MikroBUS-A, self-clocked framed master) | existing `tdm_smoke` demo |
| BCLK | SCK1 → RP75 (RE10) | unchanged |
| DATA out | SDO1 → RP101 (RG4) | unchanged |
| DATA in | SDI1 ← RP106 (RG9) | unchanged |
| FS marker | FRMSYNC (`SS1`) every 4 words, 1 SCK wide | `FRMCNT=2`, `FRMSYPW=0` |
| FS marker → CLC | `SS1` routed to **Virtual Pin RPV8** via `RP137R = _RPOUT_SS1(27)` | DS Table 12-20 |
| CLC input select | `CLC10SEL.DS1 = 0b110` = "Virtual Pin 8 Output" | DS Table 28-2 |
| CLC function | `CLC10CON.MODE = 0b110` = **J-K flip-flop with R** | DS Figure 28-3 |
| J = K = 1 | empty gates 2/4 inverted via `G2POL=G4POL=1` (J=Gate2, K=Gate4) | — |
| Reset to known state | `G3POL=1` (R=Gate3 asserted) at enable, then `G3POL=0` (released) | — |
| External FS pin | `RP70 = _RPOUT_CLC10OUT(78)` (was `SS1`) | device header |
| FS polarity | `CLC10CON.LCPOL` (`APP_TDM_MASTER_FS50_CLC10_INVERT`) | — |

`MODE32=1`, `FRMEN=1`, `AUDEN=0` (framed SPI, **not** Audio mode) are unchanged from the
normal TDM8 master.

### CLC J-K-with-R gate roles (DS Fig 28-3, verified on HW)
`Gate1 = CLK`, `Gate2 = J`, `Gate4 = K`, `Gate3 = R` (async reset) — even gates carry the
data inputs (J/K), odd gates the controls (CLK/R). Gate1 is fed `Data1` (= RPV8, the FSYNC
marker) via `CLC10GLS.G1D1T=1`; J=K=1 via `G2POL=G4POL=1`; reset via `G3POL` (1 at init for
a known state, then 0). It toggles on the marker's rising edge.

> ⚠️ The mismapping `Gate3=K / Gate4=R` leaves **R asserted** (via `G3POL=1`), holding the
> flip-flop permanently in reset → a **static** CLC output. This was the first-cut bug,
> caught on the bench (CLC bypass passthrough proved the routing, isolating it to the FF).

## Enabling

`src/app/app_config.h`:

```c
#define APP_TDM_MASTER_FS50_BY_CLC10      1   /* 0 = normal direct-SS1 FS (default) */
#define APP_TDM_MASTER_FS50_CLC10_INVERT  0   /* 1 = invert FS phase (CLC10 LCPOL)  */
```

`0` restores the exact original behavior (FS = SS1 direct on RP70). The data path
(DMA geometry, block callback, BCLK/DATA) is identical in both modes; only the FS-pulse
cadence and the RP70 PPS source change.

## Startup banner (UART console)

```
 [FS50] TDM Master FS mode: CLC10 50% experiment
        SPI1: framed master, MODE32=1 FRMEN=1 FRMCNT=2(=>FS/4words=128BCLK) FRMSYPW=0 AUDEN=0
        CLC10: J-K FF toggle (MODE=6) clk=SS1 marker via RPV8(RP137R), ON=1 LCPOL=0
        FS source = CLC10OUT -> RP70 (was SS1); BCLK/FS ~256, FS duty ~50%
```

## Oscilloscope checklist

Probe pins (TQFP128 net = RPn):

| Signal | Pin | Expectation |
|---|---|---|
| BCLK | RP75 (RE10) | continuous ~12.5 MHz (BRG=7); unchanged vs normal mode |
| DATA out | RP101 (RG4) | continuous TDM8 data; transfer must not stall |
| **FS (CLC10OUT)** | **RP70 (RE5)** | **~50% duty, period 256 BCLK, ≈48.8 kHz** |

Verify:
- [ ] BCLK present and unchanged.
- [ ] DATA present and continuous (no stall).
- [ ] FS on RP70 is ~50% duty (not the old narrow pulse).
- [ ] FS period = 256 BCLK; FS edge flips every 128 BCLK.
- [ ] BCLK/FS ≈ 256; FS ≈ 48.8 kHz (BCLK ≈ 12.5 MHz with current BRG=7; the brief
      ~49.15 kHz / 12.58 MHz figures in the request correspond to a slightly different BRG).
- [ ] No DMA underrun/overflow or SPI error (status line `miss=` stays 0).

To watch the **SPI marker itself** (optional), temporarily also route `SS1` to a free
debug pin (in addition to RPV8) and confirm a 128-BCLK-spaced pulse.

## Known caveats / things to confirm on the bench

- **FRMCNT=2 side effects:** FRMCNT only changes the FSYNC *pulse cadence*; the SPI
  shift/DMA is driven by buffer (RBF/TBE) events, so the data stream should be unaffected.
  This is the main thing to confirm on the scope (DATA continuity + `miss=0`).
- **Initial phase:** the CLC output is a separate signal from SS1, so it may start one
  half-frame out of phase. Flip `APP_TDM_MASTER_FS50_CLC10_INVERT` (→ `LCPOL`) if the
  receiver expects the opposite LRCK phase.
- **Slot mapping:** with FRMPOL active-high TDM framing and the init reset, FS-Low spans
  slots 0..3 and FS-High spans slots 4..7 (one half-frame offset from SS1). Confirm the
  1-bit delay / slot alignment if a real codec consumes this as I2S LRCK.
- The FS marker route is **internal** (RPV8) — no external jumper needed. (Fallback option
  B would be to route SS1 to a pad and jumper it into a `CLCINx` PPS input; not used here.)
